title: Auth Service Degraded — EU-West Session Cache Exhaustion
rca: Refresh token rotation feature deployed at 23:45 UTC introduced a 24-hour TTL for token pairs, causing Redis in eu-west-1 to store both old and new tokens during the rotation window. Over 12 hours of EU-West user activity, cache grew to 92% memory utilization, triggering LRU eviction of active sessions and producing 4-6 second latency spikes.
resolution_steps: 1. Initiated Redis key flush for expired rotation tokens at 11:23 UTC
2. Redis memory reduced to 74% — latency recovering
3. Hotfix PR: reduce rotation TTL from 24h to 1h — in review
business_impact: Approximately 30% of EU-West region users experiencing session drops. 3 enterprise accounts impacted including Salesforce integration disruption. ~2,400 affected sessions. No US-region impact.
workaround: Emergency Redis flush in progress — partially restoring service.

communication_log.note: Root cause identified — Redis double-load from token rotation TTL bug
Hotfix PR ready for review, ETA 11:50 UTC
